title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
At a house where Tina has been injured, a doctor instructs Corton to keep her warm and provide hot tea or coffee upon her waking. Mary Darland becomes agitated, shouting that she had just left Philip, who was fine and writing something, and questioning why he was so stubborn. Donald Craig enters and disputes the claim that Tina merely fainted; he declares that she was stabbed in the back and calls for an ambulance.

In a hotel room, Arthur Calgary reviews his notes, realizing he previously focused too much on Mrs. Aggles. He shifts his perspective to Jacqueline, considering various character assessments of her from Dr. McMaster, Leo Aigle, Hester, Kerstin Lindström, and Tina. He identifies a pattern involving money—specifically Mrs. Aigle’s trust fund, annuities, and bank accounts—as the "unknown factor" in the case. Calgary calls Hester, who informs him that Philip Darwent has been killed and Tina is in the hospital. Calgary promises to visit her after speaking with Chief Inspector Huishe.

Calgary meets with Inspector Huishe, who reports that Tina has woken up. Huishe explains that Philip Darwent was stabbed in the back while meddling in the murder case. He notes that the killer is among them and suggests that Tina is withholding information out of fear, possibly due to her relationship with Mike. Huishe reveals that a knife with blood on it was found in Philip's pocket. Huishe provides a timeline: at 4:00, Mary Darland was in the kitchen while Leo Aigle, Gwendolyn Vaughan, Hester Aggell, and Kerstin Lindström were in other parts of the house. At 4:05, Mickey and Tina arrived; Tina went upstairs and, along with Lindström, discovered Philip dead.
